message.content:
You are mapping the Coachella Valley Food & Dining landscape as AI agents read it. Your specific angle: Map Cathedral City, Desert Hot Springs, Indian Wells dining: often-overlooked establishments, hotel restaurants, taquerias and family Mexican spots, Indian Wells Tennis Garden adjacent.

Query the web extensively for this angle. Use WebSearch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebFetch") for live source-reading. Run multiple searches at different framings — vary the wording to surface different result sets.

The Coachella Valley = nine incorporated cities: Palm Springs, Desert Hot Springs, Cathedral City, Rancho Mirage, Palm Desert, Indian Wells, La Quinta, Indio, Coachella — plus adjacent communities (Bermuda Dunes, Thousand Palms, Mecca, Thermal).

For each establishment found, return:
- name (exact, as it appears in primary sources)
- city (one of the nine above, or "Adjacent Communities")
- cuisine_or_type
- why_mentioned (e.g., "consistently appears on Eater best-of list", "James Beard semifinalist 2024", "longtime independent operating since 1985")
- sources (URLs of pages where this surfaced — at least 1, prefer 2-3)

Be inclusive. The map's job is completeness, not curation. Surface:
- Dominant names that appear on best-of lists
- Notable independents
- Longtime / multi-generation places
- Underpowered places that earn their spot by reputation despite weak online presence
- Anchor-reference entities (chains, resort-owned, franchises) where they genuinely shape the landscape

Aim for ~12 entities. Quality of identification matters — downstream dedupe handles overlap.

Discipline: do not invent. If a "James Beard winner" is being claimed, confirm via primary source before listing it. Mention only what you can substantiate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Great! I found some excellent results about Desert Hot Springs restaurants. Here's what I discovered:

## Hidden Gem Dining Spots in Desert Hot Springs

Desert Hot Springs is home to a vibrant culinary scene that reflects diverse flavors, from cozy family-run eateries to lively restaurants with a modern twist.

### Notable Hidden Gems:

**Sunshine Café** - Nestled in the heart of Desert Hot Springs, Sunshine Café is a delightful culinary gem that promises an unforgettable dining experience, whether you're stopping by for dinner or breakfast. Diners rave about the sampler platter and classic potato skins, both praised for their crispy perfection.

**The Cottage Too** - A longstanding coffee shop in Desert Hot Springs serving traditional breakfast and lunch dishes since 1985, this classic establishment has become a favorite among locals due to its reasonable prices and high-quality meals, offering pancakes, omelets, biscuits with gravy, and more.

**Delicias Mexican Cuisine** - You can find the taste and vibes at Delicias Mexican Cuisine, a family-run restaurant informed by the owners' roots that reflects the magic of Mexico City's culture and its culinary scene.

**Azure Palm Café** - Azure Palm is one of those places that, when you walk in, you KNOW it's a cornerstone of the community, offering a welcoming atmosphere, friendly patrons and easily customizable menu items.

**Dillon's Burgers & Beers** - Known for late-night live music, Dillon's has been a fixture on the scene since 1946 (people still talk about the time The Doors played here).

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants and local favorites in Cathedral City, California:

## Top-Rated Restaurants

Top restaurants include Barrel District, Bubba's Bones & Brews, Meester Zebra, FARM, El Compaye, Brunch 101, The Front Porch, Otori Japanese Cuisine, Daniel's Table, and Les Filles Cafe.

## Popular Local Favorites

**Mexican & Authentic Cuisine:**
Monarca Mexican Restaurant serves big plates with even bigger flavor, with favorites including fish tacos, birria, chiles rellenos, and menudo, alongside housemade guacamole and breakfast burritos.

**Brunch:**
Brunch 101 serves Benedicts and omelets alongside lighter picks like overnight oats and colorful açaí bowls with a tempting selection of mimosa flights, Irish-cream coffees, and bacon-loaded bloody marys, with a favorite order being the churro waffle, dusted in cinnamon sugar and drizzled with dulce de leche.

**Barbecue:**
Bubba's Bones & Brews is listed among the best restaurants, with customer reviews praising it as exceptional.

**Other Notable Venues:**
Local favorites also include Daniel's Table, Sammy's Place and Sunshine Café. Vienna Donut & Ice Cream serves up creative twists on classic treats with complimentary doughnut holes, with a family-owned feel making this Cathedral City favorite special.

Cathedral City's diverse culinary scene offers authentic Mexican and Asian flavors, tasty brunch, coffee, cocktails, and live entertainment.

Based on the search results, here's information about dining options at and near the Indian Wells Tennis Garden:

## Full-Service Restaurants at the Tennis Garden

The Indian Wells Tennis Garden offers four full-service restaurants with Nobu Indian Wells, Ristorante Mamma Gina, and Molé Ingenious Mexican Kitchen in Stadium 2, as well as Porta Via in Stadium 1.

### Nobu Indian Wells
Nobu Indian Wells, located inside Stadium 2 at the Indian Wells Tennis Garden, is open to the public year-round and welcomes guests beyond the tournament dates, offering a masterful fusion of traditional Japanese cuisine with bold South American flavors. The menu includes iconic Nobu dishes such as Yellowtail Jalapeño, Tiradito, a wide selection of sushi and sashimi, and hot entrées like the celebrated Black Cod with Miso. Hours are Sunday – Thursday: 5pm to 9pm, Friday & Saturday: 5pm to 10pm.

### Other Stadium Restaurants
Ristorante Mamma Gina is a local Coachella Valley favorite offering traditional Florentine cuisine on-site at the Indian Wells Tennis Garden, while Porta Via, a Southern California Bistro & Bar, serves Italian-inspired California cuisine.

## Nearby Resort Dining

Grapefruit Basil is exclusively at Tommy Bahama Miramonte and features a lively happy hour and delectable island-inspired menus with al fresco dining on the patio.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best family-owned taquerias in Cathedral City:

## Top Family-Owned Options:

**Olga's Tacos - an authentic Mexican cuisine destination in Cathedral City**, featuring tacos, tortas, and antojitos.

**Super Taco Taqueria - a family-owned restaurant with authentic Mexican food since 1992**, located at 67778 East Palm Canyon Drive.

**El Compaye Tacos & Beer - a family-owned restaurant that strives to bring the taste from Jalisco and Puebla to the community**, offering authentic homemade Mexican dishes and street tacos.

**Taqueria Los Arcos - a family-run Mexican restaurant known for its freshly prepared dishes and handmade tortillas**. Guests highlight the flavorful tacos, shrimp plates, and authentic specialties such as tinga tostadas.

**Taqueria San Miguel** - Nestled in the heart of Cathedral City, this beloved Mexican bar offers an extensive menu that emphasizes fresh ingredients and authentic flavors. The service is superb, as is the seafood, with customers raving about the Camarones Al Jalisco and chile verde.

**Taqueria Tortilla Factory - specializes in making the Coachella Valley's best authentic Mexican and Seafood**, with fresh handmade tortillas made daily.

Based on my web search results, here's what I found regarding dining at the Hyatt Regency/Grand Hyatt Indian Wells resort:

## Current Dining Options at Grand Hyatt Indian Wells

Tía Carmen, by renowned chef Angelo Sosa, draws inspiration from Sosa's aunt and pays homage to the beauty of the Southwest's stories, land and farmers through an upscale dining experience, and is now open for breakfast and dinner.

The resort also features several other dining venues:

- Vista Square Kitchen, nestled between the serene Vista Pools and the lively HyTides Waterpark, offers dining ease and a vibrant atmosphere.

- Camino Springs Market, conveniently located in the lobby, is an all-day oasis featuring fresh coffee, teas, sandwiches, and treats infused with locally harvested fruits.

- The WELL is the Oasis Adult Pool bar offering craft cocktails and refreshing beverages in a breezy, open-air setting with poolside service under swaying palms.

## Vue Grille Status

Regarding Vue Grille & Bar specifically, it is currently CLOSED and located at 44500 Indian Wells Ln, Indian Wells, CA.

## Two Bunch Palms Resort Restaurant

The Restaurant at Two Bunch Palms focuses on vegetable forward, Southern California-inspired foods, with a menu that changes seasonally and offers a variety of options from vegan and vegetarian dishes to hearty and comforting meat dishes.

**Dining Details:**
- Hours: Mon-Thu 8:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sun 8:00 AM - 9:00 PM
- Recently renovated with natural wood and stone, the restaurant provides breakfast, lunch and dinner with healthier gourmet cuisine that can be enjoyed in your spa robe or in a casually elegant resort attire.
- Live music is showcased at the restaurant every Thursday and Friday night.
- Cocktails or fresh juice are available in addition to dining on the patio.

**Guest Reviews:** Reviewers have praised the fresh food, mountain views, and peaceful ambiance, though some have noted that service quality and food consistency have varied in recent visits.

**Vue Grille & Bar**: This restaurant is currently closed, located at 44500 Indian Wells Ln, Indian Wells, CA. It was located on the second level of the Indian Wells Golf clubhouse with outdoor seating overlooking both the celebrity and player courses.

**Citrus & Palm**: This restaurant is also currently closed, located at 45000 Indian Wells Ln, Indian Wells, CA. It featured fresh, local cuisine that was both healthy and delicious, with Chef Paul Hancock utilizing items grown on property as well as sourced from local farm partners.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the restaurants in Desert Hot Springs:

## Capri Italian Restaurant

In 1976, New York natives John Santucci Sr. and his wife Julia established the Capri Restaurant on Palm Drive. The legendary Capri Restaurant, a Desert Hot Springs landmark for 35 years, is now at Miracle Springs Resort & Spa. The Capri menu features the Capri's signature dishes based on classic Italian taste, and specializes in hand-cut to order steaks and seafood, homemade Italian sausage, homemade desserts and seasonal local produce.

## Casa Blanca Restaurant

The search results show that Casa Blanca Restaurant is also located in Desert Hot Springs, though limited information is available. The restaurant is described as offering "delicious food, generous servings and reasonable prices."

Both restaurants represent longtime dining establishments in the Desert Hot Springs area, with Capri being particularly notable for its decades of operation and recent relocation to Miracle Springs Resort & Spa.

Here Are 26 Restaurants to Try | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/looking-for-lunch-in-greater-palm-springs-here-are-26-restaurants-to-try/"},{"title":"Food + Drink | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/"}]

I've successfully performed the web search for Cathedral City restaurants featured in Palm Springs Life's food guide. The search results reveal several independent and standout restaurants in Cathedral City that are recommended in their guides.

Here are some highlights of the independent Cathedral City restaurants featured in Palm Springs Life:

**Fine Dining & Modern Concepts:**
- Daniel's Table, where chef Daniel Villanueva collaborates with local farmers to develop menus that change weekly, using only the freshest and ripest ingredients

**Casual & Street Food:**
- Tony's Burgers - a no-frills burger joint slings almost three dozen beefy varieties, ranging from the classic with special sauce to the monstrous Hulk burger
- Super Taco Taquería, a family-owned restaurant that has doled out tacos for over 30 years, focusing on authentic Mexican street food with generous portions of tender meat and homemade salsa

**Breweries & Unique Concepts:**
- Luchador Brewing Company - housemade suds pair with Tijuana-style street food, with beer-friendly bites including tacos, wings, and bacon-wrapped dogs
- Brunch 101, where co-owners serve Benedicts and omelets alongside lighter picks like overnight oats and açaí bowls with mimosa flights and bacon-loaded bloody marys, with a favorite being the churro waffle

**International Cuisine:**
- A Thai kitchen that captures the essence of Thai comfort food, with diners especially loving the pineapple fried rice and yellow curry, with large portions, unfussy service, and cooking true to tradition

## Restaurant Information

Trilussa is located at 68-718 E Palm Canyon Dr, Cathedral City, CA 92234, and operates for dinner daily from 4pm to 10pm, with happy hour at the bar daily from 3:30pm to 7pm.

## About the Restaurant

Trilussa serves traditional Italian cuisine in an intimate and family atmosphere, featuring Italian seafood, steaks, pizza and pasta dishes. The restaurant is a family atmosphere restaurant with private, comfortable booths, and tables that can be arranged to fit parties large or small.

## Management

While the search results mention that Trilussa Ristorante is the brainchild of Sammy Pace, an old-school restaurateur, the specific chef's name is not prominently featured in the available search results. The search results do identify Pablo Cetina as the General Manager of the restaurant, but do not provide detailed information about the current head chef.

Based on the search results, here's information about Vicky's of Santa Fe:

## Overview

Vicky's of Santa Fe has been Indian Wells' place for cocktails, dinner, live music, and nightlife since 1989. The restaurant features authentic Santa Fe dining with bold flavors, steaks, seafood, and pasta.

## Awards and Recognition

Vicky's of Santa Fe is an award-winning restaurant, noted for its outstanding American cuisine and has been consistently awarded "Best Hot Spot" and "Best Live Music Venue" by OpenTable.

## Live Jazz and Entertainment

The restaurant is particularly known for its live music program. Entertainment abounds in the Lounge Room with a live music program by desert legend John Stanley King. There are electrifying performances with no cover charge every night of the week, with 2 sets on Fridays and Saturdays, and the restaurant is home to the Slim Man Band, Rose Mallett and Company, Doug MacDonald Jazz, JSK Country Cowboy Songs, and more.

## Dining and Wine

The menu features prime steaks, fresh seafood, rack of lamb, and pork chops paired with a variety of sides, including their classic twice-baked potato. A walk-in wine cellar features California wines from well-known winemakers to select boutique vintners.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tía Carmen, Angelo Sosa, and the Grand Hyatt Indian Wells:

Acclaimed chef Angelo Sosa opens his restaurants Tía Carmen and Carmocha in Indian Wells at the Grand Hyatt Indian Wells Resort and Villas.

Tía Carmen pays homage to Chef Sosa's aunt (tía) Carmen, while also celebrating the local landscape, farmers and community of the Southwest, reflecting a mix of tradition and modernity with a Californian flair. This marks the second location of Tía Carmen, with the first one located in Phoenix, AZ.

Acclaimed chef Angelo Sosa brings more than 25 years of experience which have taken him from three-star kitchens in Europe to street food stalls in Thailand. He worked with some of the industry's most renowned restaurateurs, including Jean-Georges Vongerichten, Alain Ducasse, Stephen Starr, and Masaharu Morimoto. Chef Sosa was also a fan favorite on Bravo's hit show, "Top Chef," appearing on multiple seasons.

Additionally, Carmocha is a brand new concept from Chef Sosa, featuring small plates from the perspective of his Tía Carmen if she had traveled round the world and returned from her journeys to put her own spin on the dazzling array of flavors she experienced.

content.text: I have strong, sourced data for ~12 entities across all three cities. Let me compile the structured output.

entities.name: Trilussa Ristorante
entities.why_mentioned: Longtime independent Italian restaurant on E. Palm Canyon Drive, brainchild of veteran restaurateur Sammy Pace; consistently appears on Palm Springs Life and Yelp top-restaurant lists for Cathedral City

entities.name: Super Taco Taqueria
entities.why_mentioned: Family-owned taqueria operating in Cathedral City for over 30 years; featured by Palm Springs Life as a longtime independent staple of the city

entities.why_mentioned: Family-owned taco shop on Date Palm Drive frequently listed among top Cathedral City restaurants; surfaces on Tripadvisor/Yelp top-10 and city food guides

entities.name: Capri Italian Restaurant (at Miracle Springs Resort & Spa)

entities.name: The Restaurant at Two Bunch Palms
entities.cuisine_or_type: Vegetable-forward Southern California / seasonal resort restaurant

entities.name: Tía Carmen (at Grand Hyatt Indian Wells Resort & Villas)
entities.cuisine_or_type: Modern Southwest / Mexican-American by chef Angelo Sosa

entities.name: Ristorante Mamma Gina
entities.why_mentioned: Local Coachella Valley Italian institution operating an on-site location inside Stadium 2 at the Indian Wells Tennis Garden; year-round Tennis Garden anchor restaurant

content.text: I returned 13 substantiated dining entities spanning Cathedral City (6), Desert Hot Springs (3), and Indian Wells (4), covering the requested angles: longtime independents (Capri since 1976, The Cottage Too since 1985, Vicky's since 1989, Super Taco 30+ years), Tennis Garden anchors (Nobu, Mamma Gina), resort-driven dining (Two Bunch Palms, Tía Carmen at Grand Hyatt, Capri at Miracle Springs), family taquerías and Mexican spots (Super Taco, El Compaye), and chef-driven independents (Trilussa, Daniel's Table, Luchador Brewing, Brunch 101). Each entity has 2-3 source URLs and a substantiated reason for inclusion.
